steps.title: Identify angle relationships from parallel lines
steps.content:
Since line $t$ intersects parallel lines $r$ and $s$:
  • Angles $b$ and $c$ form a linear pair at line $s$ (adjacent angles on a straight line), so they are supplementary: $b + c = 180°$
  • Angles $a$ and $c$ are corresponding angles—they occupy the same relative position at each intersection—so they are equal: $a = c$
Combining these relationships: $a + b = 180°$

steps.title: Solve for k
steps.content:
Substitute the expressions for $a$ and $b$ into $a + b = 180°$:
$$(4k + 57) + (\frac{k}{4} + 55) = 180$$
Combine like terms:
$$4k + \frac{k}{4} + 112 = 180$$
$$\frac{16k + k}{4} = 68$$
$$\frac{17k}{4} = 68$$
$$k = 16$$
